payload.answer: Rejected: the proposal is an excessively granular table-to-card conversion and should not be committed as 500 durable memory cards.

entity_resolution.reason: The input describes 500 atomized rows/cards without any trustworthy, durable, or clearly non-redundant facts to commit as a memory set; preserving ambiguity and avoiding overcommitment is required.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether a proposed set of 500 CSV-derived memory cards should be committed to durable memory.

details: No durable memory cards were committed.
The proposal was rejected due to large-table atomization risk.
Ambiguous or redundant row-level facts were not promoted to memory.

repair_options: Collapse repeated CSV rows into a smaller set of stable, non-overlapping facts.
Commit only high-signal, durable summary statements rather than one card per row.
Deduplicate near-identical rows and preserve uncertainty where the source is ambiguous.
Separate transient table structure from actual user-relevant memories.
